pi_items.source: VitalDB public dataset contains 6,388 cases (case IDs 1-6388). Case 6 is a real surgical patient case from the VitalDB dataset published in Scientific Data journal (Nature) in 2022. Confirmed via: https://physionet.org/content/vitaldb/1.0.0/ and https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-022-01411-5

Verifier contains hardcoded fallback value 'HR=66.0' for t=900s as an 'APPROXIMATE KNOWN VALUE'. Unable to independently verify against actual VitalDB Case 6 data due to: (1) vitaldb Python library requires authentication login, (2) public API endpoints return 403/404, (3) vital file download from physionet.org times out. The value is physiologically plausible (within 60-100 bpm normal range for surgical patient) but cannot be confirmed as accurate.
Verifier contains hardcoded fallback value 'SBP=108.0' for t=900s. Unable to independently verify - see notes for HR. Value is physiologically plausible but unverified.

Verifier contains hardcoded fallback value 'DBP=54.0' for t=900s. Unable to independently verify. Note: This value (54 mmHg) is slightly below the typical minimum of 60 mmHg for non-shocked patients, suggesting possible intraoperative hypotension, which is physiologically plausible during anesthesia.
Verifier fallback value. Cannot independently verify - see HR_900s notes.
Verifier fallback value. Cannot independently verify.
Verifier fallback value. DBP=48 is notably low (normal minimum ~60), suggesting significant hypotension during this timepoint.
Verifier fallback value. Cannot independently verify.
Verifier fallback value. Cannot independently verify.
Verifier fallback value. DBP=56 is also slightly low.

Path is specified in task metadata and used in verifier.py export logic. Cannot verify without executing task on Windows environment. Appears to be a standard Windows Documents folder path for user 'Docker' (which is the default username in the Windows VM environment used by this task suite).

Verifier code at line 219 uses: 'if hr_diff <= 5 and sbp_diff <= 10 and dbp_diff <= 10'. Metadata claims tolerance_raw: 5. This matches the HR tolerance (±5 bpm) but SBP/DBP use ±10. The metadata value of 5 appears to be for HR/DBP tolerance, not a general 'raw' tolerance.
Verifier code at line 198 uses: 'if abs(calc_si - reported_si) > 0.1'. This exactly matches the tolerance_index metadata value of 0.1 for Shock Index.
Verifier code at line 202 uses: 'if abs(calc_pp - reported_pp) > 5'. This exactly matches the tolerance_pp metadata value of 5 mmHg for Pulse Pressure.

VitalDB is a publicly available, peer-reviewed dataset of high-resolution vital signs from 6,388 surgical patients, published in Scientific Data (Nature) in 2022. It is real-world data, not synthetic.

privileged_info_summary: VitalDB Case 6 is a real surgical patient case from the VitalDB dataset published in Scientific Data journal. The task asks agents to read vital signs (HR, SBP, DBP) at three surgical timepoints (15m, 45m, 75m) and calculate two hemodynamic indices: Shock Index (HR/SBP) and Pulse Pressure (SBP-DBP). The verifier uses hardcoded ground truth values (HR: 66/72/75 bpm, SBP: 108/96/112 mmHg, DBP: 54/48/56 mmHg at the three timepoints) that are marked as 'fallback approximations' in the code. These values are physiologically plausible for an intraoperative patient but cannot be independently verified against actual VitalDB Case 6 data due to authentication/access limitations. The tolerance parameters (HR ±5 bpm, SBP ±10 mmHg, DBP ±10 mmHg, Shock Index ±0.1, Pulse Pressure ±5 mmHg) are correctly reflected in the verifier code.
